name: Basal Ganglia
location: Deep subcortical grey matter masses surrounding the thalamus.
description: Subcortical nuclei cluster (Striatum, Globus Pallidus, Subthalamic Nucleus, Substantia Nigra) selecting, initiating, and refining voluntary motor programs and habits.
clinicalImportance: Degeneration of dopaminergic cells in Substantia Nigra causes Parkinsonian tremors and bradykinesia.
researchNotes: Operates via parallel Direct ('GO') D1-receptor pathway and Indirect ('STOP') D2-receptor pathway.
interestingFacts: Acts as a neural filter selecting 1 intended motor movement out of thousands of candidate actions.
